title: A cross-geometry scaling law for pulsed plasma plume steering
abstract: We report a scaling law for pulsed plasma plume steering and show that it predicts thrust-vector response across three laboratory geometries relative to an analytic plume-steering model. Figure 1 shows cross-geometry agreement, Table 1 reports uncertainty intervals, and the discussion defines the low-power failure regime below 8 W. Data, code, and calibration logs are publicly archived.
manuscript_text: We drive three laboratory plume-steering geometries with the same pulsed source and evaluate the observed thrust-vector response against an analytic plume-steering model across matched operating windows. Figure 1 shows agreement between the measured response and the scaling-law prediction for each geometry, and Table 1 reports uncertainty intervals for the fitted exponents and residuals. The manuscript states the exact low-power failure regime below 8 W, where the scaling law no longer holds, and positions the result against prior plasma-thruster steering references with explicit scope limits for the laboratory apparatus.
figures_and_captions: Figure 1. Cross-geometry agreement between the measured thrust-vector response and the scaling-law prediction.
tables: Table 1. Fitted exponents, residuals, and uncertainty intervals for the three laboratory geometries.
references: Ref1 analytic model for pulsed plasma plume steering.
Ref2 laboratory geometry comparison for electric propulsion steering.
Ref3 uncertainty quantification for thrust-vector measurements.
supplement_or_appendix: Appendix A lists the pulsed-source timing, diagnostic calibration logs, and geometry-specific operating windows.
data_availability: Laboratory measurements and calibration logs are archived in the supplementary repository.
code_availability: Fitting and analysis scripts are archived with the calibration logs.
materials_availability: Geometry dimensions and diagnostic settings are listed in Appendix A.
ethics_and_disclosures: The authors declare no competing interests.
